Legal

Terms of Use

InfyniPay B.V. · Last updated: June 2026

Summary: InfyniPay provides its services on an "as is" basis. Merchants are responsible for their own compliance with applicable laws. We may suspend service for violations. We do not hold customer funds. Dutch law governs this agreement.

1. Overview & Definitions

These Terms of Use govern your access to and use of the services provided by InfyniPay B.V., a company incorporated under Dutch law with its registered office in Amsterdam, Netherlands ("InfyniPay", "we", "us", or "our").

By accessing or using our website, platform, or any related service — including PaymentHood — you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, you must not use our services.

  • InfyniPay B.V. — the company that owns and operates the InfyniPay platform and PaymentHood.
  • Merchant — any business or individual using InfyniPay or PaymentHood to accept payments from their customers.
  • Client / End-customer — the person making a payment through a Merchant's integration.
  • Platform / Service — the PaymentHood SaaS payment orchestration system and all associated tooling, APIs, and dashboards.

2. Eligibility

To use our services you must be at least 18 years of age and legally capable of entering into a binding contract. By registering an account you represent and warrant that you meet these requirements.

Merchants registering on behalf of a legal entity represent that they have the authority to bind that entity to these Terms.

3. Account & Billing Information

You are responsible for maintaining the accuracy and security of your account credentials. You agree to provide current, complete, and accurate registration information and to promptly update that information if it changes.

InfyniPay reserves the right to refuse, cancel, or limit any transaction or account at our discretion, including where we have reason to believe activity is fraudulent, abusive, or in violation of these Terms.

4. Modifications to Service, Prices, and Terms

InfyniPay reserves the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue any aspect of the service at any time, with or without notice. Prices for services may be updated with reasonable notice. We will endeavour to notify Merchants of material changes via email or through the platform.

Continued use of the service after any modification constitutes your acceptance of the updated Terms. We recommend checking this page periodically.

5. Prohibited Uses

You agree not to use the InfyniPay platform for any of the following purposes:

  • Processing payments for illegal goods or services under applicable law;
  • Money laundering, fraud, or any other financial crime;
  • Violating any applicable EU, Dutch, or international law or regulation, including PSD2, AML directives, and GDPR;
  • Infringing the intellectual property rights of InfyniPay or any third party;
  • Submitting false, misleading, or incomplete information;
  • Uploading malicious code, malware, or scripts that could disrupt the service;
  • Harvesting personal data outside the normal scope of payment processing;
  • Attempting to reverse-engineer, decompile, or circumvent any security feature of the platform.

6. API Fair Use

Merchants must not send excessive API requests that degrade service performance for other users. InfyniPay may impose rate limits and may suspend or restrict API access for accounts that consistently exceed reasonable usage thresholds. We will provide advance warning where practicable.

7. Personal Information

Your use of our services is also subject to our Privacy Policy, which is incorporated into these Terms by reference. By using our services, you consent to the collection and use of information as described therein, in accordance with the GDPR and applicable Dutch law.

8. Errors, Inaccuracies, and Omissions

Information on the platform may occasionally contain typographical errors, inaccuracies, or omissions. InfyniPay reserves the right to correct any such errors and to update information at any time without prior notice.

9. Disclaimer of Warranties

The service is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis without warranties of any kind, either express or implied, including but not limited to implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, or non-infringement.

InfyniPay does not guarantee that the service will be uninterrupted, timely, secure, or error-free, nor that defects will be corrected.

10. Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by Dutch and EU law, InfyniPay B.V. and its directors, officers, employees, and affiliates shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages — including but not limited to loss of profits, loss of data, or business interruption — arising out of or related to your use of the service.

Our total aggregate liability for any claim arising out of or relating to these Terms shall not exceed the fees paid by you to InfyniPay in the three (3) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

11. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless InfyniPay B.V. and its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, judgements, and reasonable legal fees arising from: (a) your breach of these Terms; (b) your violation of any applicable law; or (c) unlawful conduct by your customers processed through the platform.

12. Our Role

InfyniPay operates as a SaaS payment gateway orchestration provider. We act as a technology intermediary — we do not hold, transfer, or custody merchant or customer funds. Merchants remain solely responsible for their business operations, customer relationships, compliance with payment scheme rules, and applicable regulatory requirements.

13. Merchant Compliance

Merchants are solely responsible for ensuring that their use of the platform complies with all applicable laws and regulations in the jurisdictions in which they operate, including PSD2, GDPR, card scheme rules (Visa, Mastercard), and anti-money-laundering regulations. InfyniPay may require evidence of compliance upon request.

14. Termination

Either party may terminate the service relationship at any time. InfyniPay may terminate or suspend your account immediately and without notice if you breach these Terms or if we are required to do so by law or a regulatory authority. Provisions that by their nature should survive termination (including limitation of liability, indemnification, and governing law) shall remain in effect.

15. Severability

If any provision of these Terms is held to be unlawful, void, or unenforceable, that provision will be severed from the remaining provisions, which will continue in full force and effect.

16. Entire Agreement

These Terms of Use, together with the Privacy Policy and any separate service agreements entered into with InfyniPay, constitute the entire agreement between you and InfyniPay and supersede all prior communications, understandings, and agreements relating to the subject matter herein.

17. Governing Law & Jurisdiction

These Terms are governed by the laws of the Netherlands. Any disputes arising from or related to these Terms shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts in Amsterdam, the Netherlands, without prejudice to your rights as a consumer under applicable EU law.

18. Contact

If you have questions about these Terms, please contact us at support@infynipay.com or write to InfyniPay B.V., Amsterdam, Netherlands.

InfyniPay

Payment infrastructure built for European businesses. Powering the future of digital commerce.

📍 InfyniPay B.V.
Amsterdam, Netherlands
